applies_to: any service processing EU user data regardless of where the company is based
key_rules: Users must consent before data is collected
Users have the right to access, correct, or delete their data
Data breaches must be reported within 72 hours
End-to-end encryption is strongly recommended for personal communications
relevance_to_junotalk: All EU users of JunoTalk have the right to request their data or deletion. Calls and messages should be encrypted.

applies_to: All parties on a recorded call within US jurisdiction
key_rules: One-party consent states (federal + most states): only one person on the call needs to consent to recording
Two-party (all-party) consent states: ALL parties must consent — includes California, Florida, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Nevada, New Hampshire, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Washington
Recording without consent in a two-party state is a criminal offense
relevance_to_junotalk: Always inform users before recording a call. In two-party consent states, get explicit agreement from all participants.

applies_to: Online services with users under 13 years old
key_rules: Cannot collect personal data from children under 13 without verifiable parental consent
Must have a clear privacy policy disclosing what data is collected from children
Parents can review and delete their child's data
relevance_to_junotalk: JunoTalk requires users to be 13+ at registration. Accounts showing signs of underage use should be flagged.
